# SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-3.0-only
# Copyright 2026 Canonical Ltd.

# Canonical user prompts referenced by the scenario files.
# These are kept here as a single, easy-to-scan corpus so reviewers can audit
# the eval surface without grepping through every YAML file.

[bootstrap]
- "I just cloned a repo and it has a workshop.yaml at the root. What do I run to get started?"
- "I want to set up a workshop for a Go project. What's the minimal workshop.yaml I need and how do I launch it?"
- "How do I find out what SDKs are available for adding a new tool to my workshop?"
- "I just ran workshop launch. How do I check it's actually ready and the project is mounted?"

[daily-ops]
- "My workshop is named \"dev\" and it's Ready. How do I run \"make build\" inside it?"
- "I just added a new entry under actions: in workshop.yaml. Do I need to run workshop refresh before I can use it?"
- "I have a \"tests\" action that runs my test runner. How do I pass extra flags to it from workshop run?"
- "I tried workshop exec but it says the workshop is Stopped. What now?"
- "I changed base: ubuntu@22.04 to ubuntu@24.04 in my workshop.yaml. How do I apply that?"

[interfaces]
- "I have a service running on port 8080 inside my workshop. How do I make it reachable at localhost:8080 on my host?"
- "I want code inside the workshop to connect to a Postgres server running on the host at localhost:5432. How do I wire that up?"
- "Two SDKs in my workshop both declare a plug for ~/.cache/foo and the launch fails with a conflict. How do I fix that?"
- "How do I point an existing mount plug at a different host directory after the workshop is already launched?"
- "I want my workshop to use the SSH keys from my host's ssh-agent so I can clone private repos. How?"

[parallel-envs]
- "I want to run two parallel test runs over the same code, isolated from each other. How do I set that up with workshops?"
- "I need to compare a build against ubuntu@22.04 and another against ubuntu@24.04, same code, side by side. What's the workflow?"
- "I'm done with one of the worktrees that had its own workshop. What's the right order to clean it up?"
- "I want to run two coding agents in parallel on the same repo, each in its own worktree, sharing one workshop sandbox. Can workshops do that?"

[troubleshoot]
- "My workshop refresh just failed with an error about a setup-base hook. What do I do?"
- "I ran workshop refresh --wait-on-error and now my workshop is in Waiting. How do I poke around inside before deciding what to do?"
- "I shelled in, manually fixed what was broken, and the workshop is still Waiting. How do I tell it to keep going?"
- "workshop info says my workshop is Ready but workshop warnings shows something. How worried should I be?"
- "Things are weird. Should I just do sudo snap remove workshop --purge and start over?"

[purge]
- "I'm done with my workshop. How do I remove it?"
- "I deleted my project directory without removing the workshop first. workshop list --global still shows the workshop. How do I clean up?"
- "My workshop has gotten weird with stale mounts and connections from earlier experiments. I don't want to lose the container though. What's the cleanest way to reset it?"
- "I just used lxc delete to remove an orphan container. How do I verify nothing's left behind?"

[out-of-scope]
- "I want to publish my own SDK to the SDK Store so others can use it. How do I do that?"
- "What is workshopctl and when do I use it?"
- "What's the difference between sdkcraft pack and workshop launch?"
